info.description:
A lightweight REST relay for managing TR-069 devices via the GenieACS NBI. v2.2.0 adds 25 new operational endpoints across 4 phases (CPE lifecycle, NOC support, customer self-service, GenieACS metadata) on top of the v2.1.0 baseline. 100% main-package coverage, 40/40 endpoints end-to-end verified on real ZTE F670L V9.0.10P1N12A via VPN lab (sessions 5i + 5j 2026-04-15).
See CHANGELOG.md [2.2.0] for full release notes and upstream genieacs-stack v1.3.1 blocker for customer factory-reset workflows.
info.title: GenieACS Relay API

put.description: Updates the CPE local web admin password via TR-069 SetParameterValues on `InternetGatewayDevice.UserInterface.WebPassword`. Distinct from PPPoE credentials and TR-069 ACS auth. Password is NOT echoed in the response or audit logs.

Toggles bridge / router mode on the CPE WAN connection by setting WANPPPConnection.Enable. Coarse approximation — real bridge-mode toggling varies by vendor and may require multiple parameter writes. v2.3.0 adds vendor detection.

post.description: Clears the device cache. Can clear cache for a specific device or all devices.

parameters.description: Device ID to clear cache for (omit to clear all)
parameters.name: device_id

get.description: Retrieves the wireless capability of a device (single-band or dual-band) based on ONU model detection

put.description: Updates DDNS configuration on the CPE via TR-069 SetParameterValues on the standard `Services.X_DynDNS` paths. Vendor variation is wide; v2.2.0 uses the most common paths and surfaces vendor incompatibility as a 503 from the GenieACS layer.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of devices known to GenieACS. Optional filters: model, online, pppoe_username (substring). Used by the admin UI device discovery flow. Pagination metadata: page, page_size, count (rows in this page), has_more (true when count == page_size).

parameters.description: Only include devices that are currently online
parameters.name: online

get.description: Looks up a single device by MAC address, serial number, or PPPoE username. Exactly one of the three query params must be provided. Returns 404 if no device matches. Used in the customer onboarding flow when the IP is not yet known.
get.summary: Search for a device by alternative key

get.description: Retrieves DHCP client information (connected devices) for a device identified by its IP address

post.description: Forces GenieACS to refresh the LANDevice.1 (DHCP host) subtree on the CPE. Use this when you want the side effect of "fetch fresh DHCP data" without fetching the data immediately. Read the refreshed data via GET /dhcp-client/{ip} after this returns.

Triggers a TR-069 IPPingDiagnostics task against the CPE. v2.2.0 ships ONLY the dispatch step — poll result fields via /params/{ip} after 5-15 seconds (the response includes the list of result parameter paths). Standard TR-098 path: `InternetGatewayDevice.IPPingDiagnostics.*`.

Triggers a TR-069 TraceRouteDiagnostics task against the CPE. v2.2.0 ships ONLY the dispatch step — poll result fields via /params/{ip} after 5-15 seconds (the response includes the list of result parameter paths). Standard TR-098 path: `InternetGatewayDevice.TraceRouteDiagnostics.*`.

put.description: Sets the DMZ host on the CPE WAN connection via TR-069 SetParameterValues. enabled=true requires host_ip; enabled=false clears the DMZ. v2.2.0 uses TR-098 IGD paths; v2.3.0 will add vendor detection.

post.description: Triggers a TR-069 FactoryReset RPC against the CPE identified by IP. Destructive — the CPE will lose its current PPPoE credentials, WLAN config, port-forward rules, and any other locally-stored state, then rejoin the ACS in a fresh provisioning state. The device is unreachable for 60-180 seconds after the task is applied.

Dispatches a TR-069 Download RPC against the CPE. Validates the firmware URL is HTTPS and that the host is not a private IP or metadata service (basic SSRF guard). Returns 202 + GenieACS task ID immediately so the caller can poll task status. Typical duration 60-300 seconds depending on file size and CPE link speed. **DO NOT TEST IN REAL LAB** — a wrong firmware image bricks the CPE.

get.description: Retrieves WLAN/SSID information, automatically triggering a refresh if data is not available and retrying until data is found or timeout
Returns the health status of the service. Does not require authentication.
Kubernetes liveness probe — returns 200 when the process is running. Does not check downstream dependencies.

put.description: Updates the WLAN MAC filter on the CPE via TR-069 SetParameterValues. mode allow = whitelist, deny = blacklist. Standard TR-098 path: `LANDevice.1.WLANConfiguration.{wlan}.WLANAccessControl.*`. Vendor variation may apply for the entry list format.

get.description: Prometheus exposition format for service metrics. No authentication required.

put.description: Updates the CPE NTP server list (max 5 entries) and/or timezone via TR-069 SetParameterValues. Standard TR-098 paths: `InternetGatewayDevice.Time.NTPServer{1..5}` and `InternetGatewayDevice.Time.LocalTimeZoneName`. At least one of ntp_servers or timezone must be provided.
put.summary: Set CPE NTP servers and timezone

get.description: Reads optical interface health metrics from the CPE. Vendor detection picks the correct TR-069 parameter path automatically. Use ?refresh=true to force a fresh fetch from the CPE before reading (slower but guaranteed fresh).
get.summary: Get CPE optical interface stats (TX/RX power, temperature, voltage, bias current)

parameters.description: Force refresh data from CPE before reading
parameters.name: refresh

404.description: Device not found OR optical interface not supported by this CPE model

post.description: Reads arbitrary TR-069 parameter values from the cached device tree (or dispatches a fresh GetParameterValues task with `live=true`). Each path is validated for safe characters before forwarding. Up to 50 paths per request.

put.description: Updates port forwarding rules at the given PortMapping slot indexes via TR-069 SetParameterValues. Caller specifies the index for each rule. v2.2.0 first iteration does NOT auto-create new instances — use enabled=false to disable a slot without removing it.

Updates the PPPoE username and password on the CPE via TR-069 SetParameterValues. Critical for auto-learning OLT deployments where the OLT does not push customer profile config and PPPoE termination happens on the CPE side. The device will drop and re-establish its WAN connection within ~30 seconds of the task being applied.

get.description: Reads a single GenieACS provisioning preset by name via the NBI query syntax. Returns the raw preset body as `body`. Returns 404 when no preset with that name exists.

put.description: Creates or updates a GenieACS provisioning preset via the NBI. Body is forwarded as-is.
put.summary: Create or update a GenieACS provisioning preset

parameters.description: Preset body (forwarded as-is to GenieACS NBI)
parameters.name: body

delete.description: Removes a GenieACS provisioning preset via the NBI.

put.description: Updates the per-WAN download / upload rate limit on the CPE via TR-069 SetParameterValues. At least one of download_kbps or upload_kbps must be provided. Rates of 0 clear the cap. v2.2.0 uses the standard `X_DownStreamMaxBitRate` / `X_UpStreamMaxBitRate` paths; vendor-specific QoS extensions are deferred to v2.3.0.

get.description: Kubernetes readiness probe — returns 200 when all upstream dependencies (GenieACS) are reachable, 503 when any dependency is down. Results cached with TTL to avoid probe storms.

post.description: Triggers a TR-069 Reboot RPC against the CPE identified by IP. The actual CPE reboot takes 30-90 seconds before the device reconnects to the ACS. Callers should NOT block waiting for the device to come back — this endpoint returns as soon as the task is submitted to GenieACS.

get.description: Retrieves WLAN/SSID configuration for a device identified by its IP address

post.description: Triggers a refresh of WLAN configuration data for a device. The refresh is processed asynchronously.

put.description: Updates static DHCP lease entries via TR-069 SetParameterValues. Caller specifies the slot index for each lease. Standard TR-098 path: `LANDevice.1.LANHostConfigManagement.IPInterface.1.DHCPStaticAddress.{index}.*`.

get.description: Returns last inform timestamp, computed online flag, uptime, and identification fields (manufacturer, model, software/hardware version) parsed from the cached GenieACS device tree.
get.summary: Get CPE status (last inform, uptime, identity)

put.description: Updates device tags via the GenieACS NBI. Tags are metadata only and don't trigger TR-069 RPCs. Wraps NBI `PUT /devices/{id}/tags/{tag}` and `DELETE /devices/{id}/tags/{tag}`. Tag names must match `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,64}`.
put.summary: Add and remove GenieACS device tags

get.description: Returns binary build metadata (version, commit, build_time, api_version, uptime). Does not require authentication.

post.description: Fires a TR-069 ConnectionRequest against the CPE without queuing any actual work. Used to wake a freshly-installed CPE so the first config push lands synchronously, wake an idle device for diagnostics, or probe responsiveness. Fire-and-forget — does NOT block waiting for the device to actually dial home. Typical wake takes 1-30 seconds.

get.description: Returns WAN connection state(s) for the CPE — connection type (pppoe/dhcp/static), connection status, external IP, uptime, optional PPPoE username. Walks all WANDevice / WANConnectionDevice instances so multi-WAN devices are surfaced as separate entries.

Returns the associated WiFi clients across all WLAN radios on the CPE. Distinct from `/dhcp-client/{ip}` which only sees clients that asked for DHCP — this endpoint reads the WLAN association table directly.

put.description: Updates the WiFi schedule (parental control) on the CPE via TR-069 SetParameterValues. Vendor variation is wide; v2.2.0 uses the most common vendor extension path family.

get.description: Returns per-radio WiFi statistics for the CPE — channel, tx power, bytes/packets sent and received, and error counters. Used by WiFi optimization recommendations and "my wifi is slow" customer support tickets.
Returns available WLAN slots for creating new WiFi networks based on device capability

post.description: Creates a new WLAN on an available slot with advanced configuration options

delete.description: Disables an existing WLAN configuration. This effectively "deletes" the WLAN by disabling it.

put.description: Optimizes WLAN radio settings including channel, mode, bandwidth, and transmit power. Supports partial updates.
Updates an existing WLAN configuration. Supports partial updates - only include fields you want to change.

auth_mode.description: Authentication mode: Open, WPA, WPA2, WPA/WPA2

password.description: Password for the new WLAN (required for WPA/WPA2)

ip.description: IP address assigned to the client

mac.description: MAC address of the client device

main.DeviceSearchResponse.description: Single-device search result. Returns the matching device or 404.

main.DeviceStatusResponse.description: Device status response — last inform timestamp, computed online flag, uptime, and identification fields parsed from the cached device tree.

main.DeviceSummary.description: Lightweight per-device summary for the list/search endpoints.

main.DevicesListResponse.description: Paginated device listing response. Pagination metadata is best-effort: page and page_size echo the request, has_more is true when the current page returned exactly page_size rows (i.e. there might be more on the next page).

main.DiagDispatchResponse.description: Diagnostic dispatch response — confirms the trigger task is queued, plus a list of result parameter paths the caller should poll via /params/{ip}.

main.FirmwareUpgradeRequest.description: Firmware upgrade request — file_url is required and must be HTTPS. file_size, target_filename, username, password, and command_key are optional.

command_key.description:
CommandKey is an optional client correlation tag echoed back in
TransferComplete events. ≤ 256 chars.

file_size.description:
FileSize is optional. When provided, lets the CPE pre-allocate
storage and reject trivially malformed payloads.

file_type.description:
FileType defaults to "1 Firmware Upgrade Image" if blank. Other
TR-069 standard values are passed through verbatim — see Annex G
of the TR-069 spec for the full list.

file_url.description:
FileURL must be HTTPS. Plaintext HTTP is rejected to avoid
MITM firmware swaps.

target_filename.description: TargetFilename is optional. CPE chooses if blank.

username.description:
Username/Password are optional HTTP basic credentials for
fetching the file from a private firmware mirror.

main.FirmwareUpgradeResponse.description: Firmware upgrade response — returns the GenieACS task ID and an estimated duration so callers can poll the task status.

main.GenericParamsRequest.description: Generic parameter passthrough request — list of TR-069 parameter paths to read from the cached device tree, plus an optional `live` flag to trigger a fresh GetParameterValues task before reading.

main.GenericParamsResponse.description: Generic parameter passthrough response — map of path → string value for found parameters, plus a list of paths that did not exist in the device tree.

bias_current_ma.description:
BiasCurrentMA — laser driver bias current in milliamps. Normal
range is roughly 5-30 mA depending on optics module. Rising bias
over time indicates laser aging.

fetched_at.description:
FetchedAt — RFC3339 timestamp of when the data was read from
GenieACS. Combine with `?refresh=true` for guaranteed freshness.

health.description:
Health — categorical classification derived from RxPowerDBm using
configurable thresholds. Values: "good", "warning", "critical",
"no_signal", "unknown".

rx_power_dbm.description:
RxPowerDBm — optical receive power in dBm. Normal PON ONU range
is roughly -8 to -27 dBm depending on splitter ratio + distance.
Below -27: marginal. Below -30: no signal (fiber broken/disconnected).
Above -8: overloaded (CPE may be too close to OLT or splitter ratio wrong).

source.description:
Source — which parameter tree the values came from. Useful for
debugging and vendor-mix analytics. Possible values:
"zte_ct_com_epon", "zte_ct_com_gpon", "huawei_hw_debug",
"realtek_epon", "standard_tr181".

temperature_c.description:
TemperatureC — internal optics module temperature in Celsius.
Normal range is 0-80°C. Above 85°C may trigger thermal shutdown.

tx_power_dbm.description:
TxPowerDBm — optical transmit power in dBm. Normal PON ONU range
is roughly -1 to +5 dBm. Lower values may indicate laser bias
degradation or power supply issues.

voltage_v.description:
VoltageV — optics module supply voltage in Volts. Normal range
is 3.0-3.6V (3.3V nominal). Out-of-range = power supply issue.

mode.description: WiFi standard mode (b, g, n, b/g, g/n, b/g/n for 2.4GHz; a, n, ac, a/n, a/n/ac for 5GHz)

main.PingDiagRequest.description: IPPingDiagnostics request — host is required (target IP or DNS name), count and timeout_ms are optional with sensible defaults.

main.PortForwardRule.description: One port forwarding rule. Index is the TR-069 PortMapping instance number (1-based). Use enabled=false to disable a slot without removing it.

main.ReadinessResponse.description: Readiness probe response including per-dependency health

data.description: Response payload (success) OR error detail (string/field list)

status.description: "success" for 2xx, HTTP reason phrase for errors

main.SSIDForceResponse.description: Force SSID response with retry information

main.SetAdminPasswordRequest.description: Admin web password update request. The password is the new value to set on the CPE's local web admin interface — it does NOT change PPPoE credentials (use /pppoe/{ip} for that) or TR-069 ACS auth.

main.SetAdminPasswordResponse.description: Admin web password update response. Does NOT echo the password value back to keep it out of relay audit logs.

main.SetBridgeModeRequest.description: Bridge mode toggle. enabled=true puts the CPE in pure bridge mode (PPPoE termination on customer router). enabled=false reverts to router mode.

main.SetDDNSRequest.description: DDNS configuration. provider, hostname, username, password are required when enabled=true.

main.SetDDNSResponse.description: DDNS configuration response. Does NOT echo username/password back.

main.SetDMZRequest.description: DMZ host configuration. enabled=true requires host_ip; enabled=false clears the DMZ. wan_instance defaults to 1.

main.SetMACFilterRequest.description: MAC filter rule update. mode = "allow" (whitelist) or "deny" (blacklist). wlan_index defaults to 1.

main.SetNTPRequest.description: NTP / timezone update request. At least one of ntp_servers or timezone must be provided.

main.SetPPPoERequest.description: PPPoE credential update request — username and password are required, wan_instance is optional and defaults to 1.

main.SetPPPoEResponse.description: PPPoE credential update response — confirms the device, IP, and which WAN instance was updated.

message.example: PPPoE credentials updated. Device will reconnect within 30s.

main.SetPortForwardingRequest.description: Set port forwarding rules. Caller specifies the rule indexes. v2.2.0 does NOT auto-create new instances — see V2.2.0-DESIGN.md §Phase 4.

main.SetQoSRequest.description: Bandwidth rate-limit request. At least one of download_kbps or upload_kbps must be provided. Rates of 0 mean "no limit" (clears the cap). wan_instance defaults to 1.

main.SetStaticDHCPRequest.description: Set static DHCP leases. Caller specifies slot indexes.

main.SetTagsRequest.description: Tag management — add and remove are both optional but at least one must be non-empty. Tags must match [a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,64}.

main.SetWifiScheduleRequest.description: WiFi schedule update — list of per-day on/off windows. wlan_index defaults to 1.

main.StaticDHCPLease.description: One static DHCP lease — binds a MAC address to a fixed IP.

main.TraceRouteDiagRequest.description: TraceRouteDiagnostics request — host is required, max_hops and timeout_ms are optional.

main.WANConnectionInfo.description: One WAN connection's state — type (pppoe/dhcp/static), connection status, external IP, uptime, optional username (PPPoE only).

main.WANConnectionsResponse.description: WAN connection state response — array of WAN connections (some CPE models expose multiple WANPPPConnection / WANIPConnection instances for multi-WAN or dual-stack).

enabled.description: TR-069 Enable flag (raw); true = slot actively broadcasting

hidden.description: True if SSID is not broadcast

ssid.description: Network name broadcast by the WLAN

main.WiFiClientInfo.description: Associated WiFi client — distinct from a DHCP lease in `/dhcp-client/{ip}` which only sees clients that asked for DHCP. WiFi clients are read from the WLAN association table on the CPE radio.

main.WiFiClientsResponse.description: Associated WiFi clients per radio. Distinct from `/dhcp-client/{ip}` which only sees clients that asked for DHCP.

main.WiFiRadioStats.description: Per-radio WiFi statistics — channel, tx power, byte/packet counters, and error rates.

main.WiFiStatsResponse.description: Per-radio WiFi statistics for the CPE.

main.WifiScheduleEntry.description: WiFi schedule entry — day of week (0=Sun..6=Sat), start_time / end_time HH:MM, enabled flag.

ApiKeyAuth.description: API key for authentication (required when MIDDLEWARE_AUTH=true)
ApiKeyAuth.name: X-API-Key
